Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (27): Pass by Value or Pass by Reference?
Python
JavaScript
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle

We're almost at the end of the iThome Ironman series, and I just realized I skipped something kind of important — pass by value vs pass by reference. So this post is a quick chat about how values and references work in Python, and how they line up with what you already know from JavaScript.
/en/python/2021/09/25/python-from-js-perspective-24-inheritance/
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (24) - Inheritance

Last time I teased that inheritance was coming up next, so of course this post is all about inheritance. We start with a quick JavaScript refresher (both the prototype version and the ES6 class version), then jump into how Python does it — spoiler: there's no extends keyword, and super works a lot like the one you already know.
/en/python/2021/09/23/learning-python-from-javascript-22-github-api/
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (22): Hitting the GitHub API

We've picked up a fair bit of Python by now, so it's about time we actually built something with it — that's how the concepts really stick. In this one we'll wire up the GitHub API with the requests package, let the user type in any GitHub username, pull back the repos that account has starred, and dump the result into a JSON file.
/en/python/2021/09/21/learn-python-from-javascript-20-csv/
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (20) - CSV

Last time we talked a bit about JSON. This time I want to add a quick chapter on handling CSV files. You'll run into CSV all the time when you do bulk imports, so it's worth pulling out into its own post. Here's how reading and writing CSV works in Python.
/en/python/2021/09/16/learn-python-from-javascript-15-modules/
Learn Python From a JavaScript Angle (15): Modules
Learn Python From a JavaScript Angle

Let's talk about modules in Python. JavaScript has its own idea of modules too, so we'll compare the two side by side — how to split your code into a separate file, export it, and import it back. Plus a couple of handy built-ins, dir() and help(), that make exploring modules way easier.
/en/python/2021/09/15/learning-python-from-javascript-14-bmi-calculator-2/
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (14) - Building a BMI Calculator, Part 2

We've wrapped up a chunk of the fundamentals, so now it's time to put those little knowledge points to work. In this one we take the BMI calculator from earlier and add some real features — terminal input, a fake five-second Loading animation, and colored output text in the terminal.
/en/python/2021/09/08/learn-python-from-javascript-07-conditionals-and-loops/
Learn Python From a JavaScript Angle (7) — Conditionals and Loops

Next up: conditionals and loops, since you basically can't write any program without these two. We'll line up JavaScript's if/else against Python's if/elif/else, talk about the new match statement in Python 3.10, and cover for and while loops including how to fake a do...while in Python.
/en/python/2021/09/06/learn-python-from-javascript-05-functions/
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (5) - Functions
Learn Python From JavaScript

Last time we talked about types and variables, so this time there's no dodging functions. Functions are a real sticking point for a lot of beginners, so I'll share a simple way to think about when you should reach for a function, plus how to practice writing them — all coming at it from a JavaScript angle.
/en/python/2021/09/04/python-from-js-03-installation/
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (3): Installing It
Homebrew

Next up is actually installing Python — because if we want to learn Python, we kind of need Python on our machine first. The install looks trivial, but Windows and macOS each have their own quirks and a couple of gotchas, so I'm giving both of them their own walkthrough here.
